content.full_text:
Mr. Wayne Imberi  
California Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street  
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Imberi:

This letter is a request, per our telephone conversation of July 19, 1990, to clarify whether or not my campaign committee must file a Form 410 (Statement of Organization) or an amended Form 410 for the November 6, 1990 election.

Friends of Ann Rock, I.D. No. 820221, first qualified as a recipient committee in the Spring of 1982, under the name Committee to Elect Ann Rock. Amended Form 410s were filed for the change in name and change of Treasurer in the 1986 election year. In the Fall of 1988, the Friends of Ann Rock campaign account was closed. All required Semi-annual Statements (Form 490) have subsequently been filed, and no Statement of Termination (Form 495) has been filed. Forms 501 and 502 now required by FPPC have also been filed.

Given the facts stated above, must Friends of Ann Rock file a Form 410 or amended Form 410 (Statement of Organization) when the one thousand-dollar threshold is reached? The directions in the FPPC manual could be construed to require such a filing. I would be most appreciative of a clarification, in writing, at your earliest convenience.

Thank you for your time and trouble.

Sincerely,

Ann H. Rock
